codeReview.summary: Clean, well-scoped tech debt cleanup across 5 files (89 insertions, 92 deletions). All four cleanup items were implemented correctly: (1) bytes_to_shaped_glyphs deduplicated into scene library as a pub function, both core and compositor now import it; (2) all unsafe blocks in scene/lib.rs have thorough SAFETY comments — verified via automated check, no missing annotations found; (3) drawing/lib.rs module doc accurately reflects alloc usage for LRU glyph cache; (4) dead types (GlyphMetrics, RasterBuffer, min_usize, scale_fu_floor) removed from truetype.rs — the canonical versions exist in shaping/src/rasterize.rs, and GlyphOutline/GlyphPoint/scale_fu/scale_fu_ceil correctly retained as they're still used. All #[allow(dead_code)] annotations removed from truetype.rs. No behavioral changes — pure refactoring with full test suite passing.

issues.description: bytes_to_shaped_glyphs is documented as 'ASCII text bytes' but actually works for any byte value (maps byte → glyph_id). The function doesn't validate ASCII range. Consider updating the doc to say 'raw text bytes' to avoid implying ASCII-only semantics. Minor accuracy nit — no behavioral impact.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly noted that the font-rendering-worker skill is oriented toward feature implementation with TDD and visual verification, making the TDD and QEMU steps inapplicable for pure cleanup/refactoring features. The skill has no guidance for cleanup features. skillFeedback suggests either a lightweight cleanup skill or a note in the existing skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'For pure cleanup/refactoring features that don't change behavior, the TDD step and QEMU visual verification steps aren't applicable.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered pre-existing tech debt in rasterizer.rs (extensive #[allow(dead_code)] annotations on Segment, ActiveEdge, RasterScratch, FP_SHIFT, etc.) that was outside the feature scope. This was correctly logged as a discoveredIssue rather than acted on, which is the right behavior. The observation is worth tracking for a future cleanup pass.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues[0]: 'rasterizer.rs in the drawing library has extensive #[allow(dead_code)] annotations on most of its types and functions'

summary: All four cleanup items implemented correctly with no regressions. bytes_to_shaped_glyphs successfully deduplicated into scene library (both consumers import from there). All unsafe blocks in scene/lib.rs have SAFETY comments (verified via automated scan). drawing/lib.rs doc accurately updated. Dead types removed from truetype.rs with live types correctly retained. Clean commit, thorough verification commands in handoff. One minor non-blocking doc accuracy nit on the bytes_to_shaped_glyphs function signature.
